decisionDrivers.title: Speed of response — right now, near me
decisionDrivers.description: The single biggest decision factor for the emergency motion. Cynthia decides in under 5 minutes. Heather & Mark and Daniel give it 1–3 hours of phone tag before defaulting to whoever picked up first. Whichever brand answers the phone, returns the form-fill within 15 minutes, and confirms a same-day or next-day visit wins the booking. Kirchner’s 24/7 emergency claim must be backed by a tap-to-call mobile experience and an LSA pack presence to convert the urgency.

decisionDrivers.title: Trust signals — license, insurance, longevity, reviews
decisionDrivers.description: Daniel reads 3+ reviews per business and checks the BBB. Heather & Mark want a Google Guaranteed badge plus 4.7☆+ reviews plus 10+ years operating. Carla requires a current COI and 2–3 commercial references. Kirchner’s real-world position (4.9☆ / 237 reviews / 40 years) is one of the strongest in the category — but only if it surfaces. Schema, BBB profile, citation consistency, and on-site licensure / insurance badges close that gap.

decisionDrivers.title: Transparent pricing & no surprise upsells
decisionDrivers.description: Daniel hates being sold a panel he doesn’t need. Vanessa wants flat-rate Tesla Wall Connector pricing she can see online before calling. Heather & Mark fear hidden install fees on a $12K generator quote. Brands that publish service-page price ranges, offer flat-rate diagnostics, and quote in writing before the truck arrives convert at materially higher rates than “call for a quote” alternatives.

decisionDrivers.title: Specialization signals for the high-stakes job
decisionDrivers.description: Generator install (Heather & Mark), FPE Stab-Lok replacement (Daniel), Tesla / EV-charger certification (Vanessa), pool / addition coordination (Michael), commercial COI & multi-property capacity (Carla). The generalist “we do all electrical work” positioning loses every one of these jobs to a brand that publishes a dedicated service page, manufacturer-certification badge, or commercial-track sub-site. Kirchner has the in-the-field expertise — the website doesn’t reflect it.

decisionDrivers.title: Local roots & neighbor recommendations
decisionDrivers.description: Heather & Mark, Daniel, and Michael all use Nextdoor and Cypress Facebook groups as primary trust validators. The 40-year operating history is a structural advantage no franchise (Mister Sparky) and no aggregator (Abacus, JMS) can match — but it has to surface. Nextdoor profile in the correct neighborhood, Facebook group presence, project photos with actual Cypress addresses, and customer-quote testimonials with neighborhood names compound this signal.

decisionDrivers.title: Mobile-first booking & speed-of-quote
decisionDrivers.description: Vanessa books from her phone without calling. Cynthia taps the LSA pack and dials the first result. Daniel form-fills at midnight and expects a response by 9am. Heather & Mark schedule the in-home generator estimate from a Saturday-morning phone session. Sticky tap-to-call, a 60-second quote-request form, and a 15-minute response SLA convert significantly better than “please complete the contact form” on a slow-loading page. The Kirchner mobile experience (20.6s LCP, no sticky CTA) is the bottleneck here.

channelMap.usageNotes: Primary discovery for every persona — “electrician cypress,” “emergency electrician near me,” “panel upgrade cypress tx,” “tesla wall connector installer,” “whole home generator cypress.” The single highest-leverage channel.

237 reviews / 4.9☆ / 40 yrs already pre-qualifies for the Cypress map pack. Currently outside it — the structural fixes (NAP, schema, brand consolidation) restore visibility. Heather/Mark, Daniel, and Cynthia all check the map pack.

Cynthia’s emergency-mode default and Heather/Mark’s urgency-trigger default. Google Guaranteed badge is the strongest trust signal at the moment of click. Kirchner is not currently in the stack — A1 Plus and Mister Sparky occupy slots #1 and #2.

Where Heather & Mark ask “anyone have a recommendation for a generator installer?” Cypress / Bridgeland / Towne Lake homeowners trust neighbor recs over almost anything else. Currently 1 recommendation — profile lives in Tomball, not Cypress.

Local Cypress homeowner groups are where Daniel reads but doesn’t post, where Heather/Mark watch hurricane-prep threads, and where Vanessa sees EV-related creator content. 535 followers + 12 reviews / 100% recommend — warm but dormant since June 2025.

Vanessa is the heaviest user (asks ChatGPT/Perplexity for shortlists). Heather/Mark cross-reference AI shortlist against Google + Nextdoor. Daniel asks “is FPE Stab-Lok safe.” Currently 2 of 6 platforms cite Kirchner. AVS 16/100.

Where Vanessa (mobile-first EV buyer) and the digitally-native portion of every persona find brands through creator content. EV-charger walkthroughs, before/after panel upgrades, generator-install reels. 105 followers, dormant since 2024.

Daniel’s vetting pattern. Yelp is verified but shows zero visible reviews and links to a legacy domain. BBB is missing entirely despite 40-year operating history. Both fixable in Phase 1; both are persistent trust signals for the careful-vetter persona.

“What is a 200-amp panel,” “why is my breaker tripping,” “cost to install a generator” — long-tail education that pre-qualifies homeowners before they call. Currently absent from Kirchner’s content stack.

Carla’s vetting channel for commercial property work. Vets via professional network, asks for 2–3 commercial references. Not a consumer-acquisition channel — but the sole channel for the highest-LTV persona in the audience set.
